title: Unable to create new rootless pods after using podman stop --all and podman rm --all
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I think I put my rootless podman into a bad state. I had a bunch of containers unrelated to my pods/containers I run on a daily basis on my workstation. I decided rather than indivudally clean everything up to do `podman stop --all` and `podman rm --all` and then relaunch my pods/containers that I wanted running via a bash shell script. After doing that I could no longer start any pods. 

```
$ podman pod ls
POD ID      NAME        STATUS      CREATED     INFRA ID    # OF CONTAINERS
$ podman pod create foo
Error: unable to create pod cgroup for pod 2e75d2c8edb0d15e52f814f6f11af9c4a68087e8342f453c80ecf83fb50f9ff6: creating cgroup user.slice/user-libpod_pod_2e75d2c8edb0d15e52f814f6f11af9c4a68087e8342f453c80ecf83fb50f9ff6.slice: Unit user-libpod_pod_2e75d2c8edb0d15e52f814f6f11af9c4a68087e8342f453c80ecf83fb50f9ff6.slice already exists.
```
I have logged out and back in and the issue continued, so I rebooted hoping it could clear up but it remains the same. 


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have a bunch of pods with containers running inside them. 
2. `podman stop --all` and `podman rm --all
3. ???
4. See you can no longer create pods.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman pod ls
POD ID      NAME        STATUS      CREATED     INFRA ID    # OF CONTAINERS
$ podman pod create foo
Error: unable to create pod cgroup for pod 2e75d2c8edb0d15e52f814f6f11af9c4a68087e8342f453c80ecf83fb50f9ff6: creating cgroup user.slice/user-libpod_pod_2e75d2c8edb0d15e52f814f6f11af9c4a68087e8342f453c80ecf83fb50f9ff6.slice: Unit user-libpod_pod_2e75d2c8edb0d15e52f814f6f11af9c4a68087e8342f453c80ecf83fb50f9ff6.slice already exists.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A pod named foo

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0-rc1
API Version:  4.3.0-rc1
Go Version:   go1.19.1
Built:        Fri Oct  7 09:31:05 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16107
TLDR some bug only present in fedora 37
